content.full_text:
Kora

A-76-228

February 17, 1975


Vino G. Nielsen, Jr.
Dobbs, Doyle & Nielsen
The Alsoa Building
One Martine Plaza, Suite 2500

Dear Mr. Nielsen:

This letter is in response to your inquiry concerning the
reporting obligations of "major donor" committees under the
Political Reform Act.

Committees which made contributions in 1975 for use in 1976
campaigns and which filed papers pursuant to Government Code
Section 84205 have satisfied their reporting responsibilities
with regard to those contributions. However, persons who qualify
as committees in 1975 who contribute to candidates and committees
to whom they made contributions or expenditures in 1975 must report the cumulative amounts of all contributions to such candidates
and committees since the filing of their last post-election statement. If they have never filed a post-election statement, since
January 7, 1975.

I apologize for the delay in this response. If we can be of
further assistance, please contact our staff again.

Sincerely,


Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

WH:bw

DOBBS, DOYLE & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

SUITE 2900, THE ALCOA BUILDING
ONE MARITIME PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111

TELEPHONE (415) 262-1940

February 11, 1976


Kenneth Finney, Esq.
Director, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Opinion on "Reporting
Requirements for Major Donors"

Dear Mr. Finney:

Enclosed please find a Xerox of our file
copy of a letter sent to the FPPC on December 24,
1975, asking for clarification of the reporting
responsibilities for major donors.

This letter was sent at the request of the
Republican State Central Committee of California,
and as of this date, we have not received a reply
to this letter. Your assistance and cooperation
is sincerely appreciated.

Very truly yours,


Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.

VGN:jy
enc.
cc: RSCCC

76 021/

December 24, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 907
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Reporting Requirements for Major Donors

Dear Commissioners:

This office is special counsel to the Republican
State Central Committee of California, which is a
committee registered pursuant to the requirements of the
Political Reform Act of 1974.

Some persons contributing to the Republican
State Central Committee of California in 1975 have
informed us that they are "major donors" as defined
by the Act, and they have asked our assistance in
complying with the disclosure provisions of the Act.
Some of these persons will again become major donors
in 1976, but others will not.

It is unclear to us what reporting deadlines
are applicable to contributors who become major donors
in 1975 by making only contributions to campaigns
involved in the 1976 election. Will the reporting of
contributions made in July of 1975 and/or January of
1976 suffice and terminate the major donor reporting
responsibilities?

Your response or opinion to this question is
sincerely appreciated.

Very truly yours,


Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.

VGN:dy
cc: Republican State Central Committee of California

(dictated but not read)
